mnemonics.title: TSL LOCK ACQUISITION
mnemonics.content: TSL = TEST SET LOCK: READ OLD, WRITE ONE, RETURN OLD. OLD IS ZERO? YOU'RE IN! OLD IS ONE? TRY AGAIN!

mnemonics.title: CAS OPERATION
mnemonics.content: CAS = COMPARE AND SWAP: LOAD EXPECTED, COMPARE TO ACTUAL, IF MATCH THEN SWAP TO NEW. IF NOT? RELOAD AND RETRY!

mnemonics.title: MEMORY FENCE TYPES
mnemonics.content: MFENCE = FULL FENCE (READS AND WRITES), SFENCE = STORE FENCE (WRITES), LFENCE = LOAD FENCE (READS)

acronyms.usage: Hardware instruction that atomically reads and modifies a lock variable for mutual exclusion
Atomic instruction that conditionally updates a value only if it matches an expected value
Pair of instructions enabling atomic read-modify-write operations on memory
Problem where a value changes from A to B and back to A between check and update, causing lost updates

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box representing lock variable. Show TSL as a combined operation: an arrow entering reads value (0), simultaneously arrow exits writing 1. Below show loop: if old=0 enter CS, if old=1 loop back.
Draw two boxes side by side: lock (0) and key (1). Show atomic swap as curved arrow exchanging their values. Result: lock=1 (locked), key=0 (unlocked). Process enters CS.
Draw three boxes: expected, actual, new. Show CAS checking if expected equals actual; if YES, new replaces actual. If NO, operation fails and must retry.
Draw Load-Linked as marking a memory location with a star. Then show Store-Conditional checking the star; if still there, write succeeds. If another process modified it, star is gone and write fails.

analogies.analogy: Like a bouncer who atomically checks if the club is empty and immediately puts a 'full' sign, all in one motion - no one can slip in between the check and the sign change.
Like updating a shared Google Doc: you see the current text, type your changes, but can only save if no one else changed it since you opened it - otherwise you must refresh and try again.
Like synchronizing notes in a group project: a fence forces everyone to write down their latest changes to the shared folder before anyone starts reading, ensuring consistent information.
Like reserving a shopping cart: you mark it as linked to you (Load-Linked), do your shopping, then try to pay (Store-Conditional); if someone took it, the payment fails and you start over.
